title: Mahabharata l Part 24 l Arjuna Kidnaps Subhadra
speaker: HG Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa

segments.text: Thank you for watching.

Om Adhyanathim Ramdhasya, Jnananjana Salakaya, Chaksharanmeelitham Jena, Tasmai Shri Gurave Namaha, Nama Om Vishnupadaya, Krishna Preshtaya Bhutale, Srimate Bhaktivedanta, Swamin Nithinamine, Namaste Saraswate Deve, Gauravani Pracharine, Nirviseshashvara,

Unyavadi, Pashyatya Deshatalini, Jaya Shri Krishna Chaitanya, Prabhu Nithyananda, Sri Advaita Gadadhara, Sri Vasari Gauravakta Vrinda, Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare,
Mookam Karoti Vachalam, Pangum Langhayati,
Jnate Girim, Yad Kripatamaham Vande, Sri Guram Deena Taranam.
And this news reached Arjuna, who happened to be on a pilgrimage and had reached Prabhasa Kshetra, which is today we know as Somnath, as part of his pilgrimage.
When he reached there, he happened to hear that Subhadra's marriage is going to get fixed with Duryodhana.
And so, Arjuna himself had a desire to marry Subhadra.
And so, therefore, he decided to go to Nagasubhadra.
Dwaraka, which was not very far away from Prabhasa Kshetra.
And he decided to go disguised as a Vaishnava Sanyasi with a tridanda.
Because he did not want to openly announce that he has come there looking for the hand of Subhadra.
So, that is how Arjuna went to Dwaraka.
And what happened there is what we will see today.
Today's episode, we begin with
the story of Arjuna.
The story of King Raivatha.
King Raivatha was the son of King Revatha.
And this King Raivatha was ruling over a kingdom in which the main city of his was located on a hill.
And because of he being the ruler of that kingdom, that hill or that mountain came to be known as Raivathaka mountain or Raivathaka hill.
This Raivatha had a daughter by the name Revaethi.
When this Revathi grew up,
she was
beautiful and well qualified
girl.
King Raivatha thought
that I have to look for a suitable groom.
For my
daughter.
Now, this King Raivatha was ruling over
his kingdom.
In the Satya Yuga.
Satya Yuga.
So, this is
many millions of years before the current proceedings which we are discussing which
happened in the Mahabharata. So this King Raivata thought, now for me to find a suitable
husband for my daughter, I have to take the advice of somebody superior. So he thought,
let me take the advice of Brahmaji himself. Because Brahmaji is the progenitor of everyone,
he is the creator, the secondary creator of this universe. So let me, because he is the
oldest personality, let me go and ask him. So King Raivata along with his daughter Revati,
he went to Brahmaloka. When he approached Brahmaloka and he came to the assembly of
Lord Brahma, he said, I want to meet you. I want to meet you. I want to meet you. I
want to meet you. I want to meet you. I want to meet you. I want to meet you. I want to meet you.
At that time, it so happened that Brahmaji was witnessing a song performance by some Gandharvas.
Actually, in the Gandharvaloka, there are five very important or most important chief Gandharvas.
One of them is Kumbhuru. And so like that, there are five people of which two of them are
haha and who who so it so happened that at that assembly of brahma ji haha and who who were
performing some musical glorification of lord vishnu lord krishna in front of brahma ji and
brahma ji was witnessing this he was participating in this so when king raivata entered brahma ji
asked him to sit down and wait till this performance gets over so king raivata along
with his daughter into they both of them sat down and for some time haha and who who were singing
the glories of krishna vishnu and once they finished then king raivata came forward to
brahma ji paid his obeisance offered him his respects and then he asked brahma ji that
uh
i
will
I want a suitable husband for my daughter raivati so i have come seeking your advice
about who is the most appropriate most suitable greatest person whom i can give my daughter
in marriage too so in brahma loka the time span is very large as we have seen twelve
hours of brahma ji is equal to forty three lakh twenty thousand into one thousand years
of our lives on this earth so without being aware of it king raivata had spent so much
time when the singing of the gandhar was going on in brahma's lord brahma's assembly on the
earth by that time what had happened satya yuga had got over satya yuga itself lasts
for more than seventeen lakh years.
So, Satyuga got over, then next was Treta Yuga which lasted for about 12 lakh years,
more than 12 lakh years, that also got over and Dwapara Yuga had almost, was almost coming
to its close, that means another almost more than 8 lakh years had already passed by.
But Raivata was not aware of this because he was sitting in Brahmaloka, the passage
of time you cannot make out.
So he was not aware of what is going on on the earth.
So Brahmaji told Raivata, the most suitable person for your daughter right now is Lord
Balarama who is an incarnation of the Supreme Lord Krishna.
So you can go and hand over your daughter in marriage to Balaramaji.
So Raivata along with his daughter came back to the earth's free planet and when he came
back he saw that the whole scenario had completely changed.
All his family members.
All his brothers, ministers, kingdom, everything had totally changed.
And one more thing what he noticed was the people had become very much diminished in size.
According to the Srimad Bhagavatam, Namsayat Aayur Balo Smriti, when time passes by from
one Yuga to another, the lifespan of the people reduces.
For example, in Satyuga people on an average, the average lifespan of the human beings was
When it came to Treta Yuga, that reduced to 10,000 years.
Dwapara Yuga... it reduced to 1000 years.
Kali Yuga, it is reduced even further to 100 years or even lesser.
So Namsayat Aayu... so Aayu reduces.
Bala... the physical strength of the people reduces.
So accordingly the size of the people also keeps diminishing.
In fact, in the past 30 years, in everybody's eyes, the size of the people has also kept
diminishing.
In South India, there is one, in Andhra Pradesh, there is one place called Lepakshi.
There is a beautiful temple there.
It is said that the place got the name Lepakshi, meaning in Telugu it means Lepakshi, means
O Pakshi, O bird, please get up.
So it is said that this is the place where Jatayu, after attacking Ravana, fell down
when he was mortally wounded by Ravana.
And when Lord Ramachandra, searching for Sita Maya, he came across Jatayu, he lifted Jatayu
in his hands and he said, Lepakshi, please get up, O bird.
So that's how this place got its name, Lepakshi.
And in that place, there is one large footprint of Sita Devi.
It is said that Ravana, after fighting with Jatayu,
rested for a little while there and that's how Sita Maya's footprint happened to get
embedded in the rock there.
That footprint, if you see, is almost maybe four feet or bigger than that.
It is that huge it is.
So from this itself, we can see the people of Satyuga, how huge their bodies were compared
to today's time.
So with time, the size of the body also keeps reducing.
Today, our feet, if you compare with Sita Devi's footprint, the mark of the footprint
that is there in Lepakshi, if we compare with that is our feet hardly not even one foot.
Whereas Sita Maya's foot is almost four feet.
So with this, we can understand how with time over the different ages, the size of the bodies
of the human beings, as well as all other animals, birds, trees.
Everything keeps changing.
Everything keeps changing.
keeps reducing.
People's Mrithi Shakti, their ability to remember, keeps reducing.
That is why in the olden days, in the Satya Yuga, Dwapara Yuga, Treta Yuga, there were
no written books.
All the students, they would go to the Gurukula, they would hear once the entire Vedic knowledge
from the Guru and they would remember it for life.
Such people are called Shruti Dhara.
They can, they have excellent memories.
Because in Kali Yuga, people cannot, don't have such intense memory, such an excellent
memory power, therefore Vedavyasa put down all the Vedas in writing for the first time.
This was 5000 years ago.
And today the situation is that whatever has been written down, that also we cannot remember.
We need computers, we need mobiles, we need these things to remind us.
So because with time, memory power keeps reducing.
So when Raivatha along with Revathi came down, he saw the size of the people had reduced
very much and he saw so many things have changed and all that.
But anyway, taking Brahmaji's advice, he approached Lord Balaram and offered his daughter
in hand to Balaram.
But Revathi was so tall and Balaram was much shorter than her.
How can he marry Revathi?
So then Balaramji, what he did, he took his plough and pulled Revathi down and reduced
their size to his height and then he married her.
So this, this story is there in folklore as well as in some Puranas, some part of the
story is also there.
This is how Lord Balaramji married Revathi.
Now we come back to our story.
Our original narration of what Arjuna was doing now that Balaram and Krishna were there
in Dwaraka.
Arjuna approached Dwaraka disguised as a sanyasi and he approached Krishna and divulged
to him that he was Arjuna and that he had come there in disguise because he in his heart
he wanted to marry Subhadra.
Now Krishna in his heart also was not keen on Subhadra marrying Duryodhana because Duryodhana's
character was not so good and Krishna, when he heard from Arjuna, Krishna was also keen
that Arjuna should marry Subhadra.
So now what to do?
It was the Chaturmasya time.
So Krishna said, you stay in Dwaraka, let us see how things develop.
So then Arjuna, once it happened, Balaram came to know that there is a travelling man.
He said, you stay in Dwaraka.
Let us see how things develop.
So then Arjuna, once it happened, Balaram came to know that there is a travelling man.
He said, you stay in the temple.
He said, you stay in the temple.
Brilliant!
Loving Tendulノ Brother Vapшi.
For Indian Sentiment, there is a travelling sanyasi Paribhrajaka who has come to our place.
He is staying with Krishna in his palace.
So he invited him the sanyasi to his palace.
Balaram ji did not know that it was Arjuna, he was in this disguise.
So Balaraj came to his.
So Arjuna, in the disguise of the sanyasi, came to Balaram s palace and Balaram had made
lot of arrangements to receive him, and he had prepared a big feast, Prasadam, for feeding
the sanyasi.

And then Balaram being very affectionate towards his sister, younger sister Subhadra, he called her to serve the sanyasi, the prasadam, so that she could get the blessings of the sanyasi by serving him.
So Subhadra came forward and Subhadra was also very beautiful, very intelligent, but at the same time she was simple at heart.
So she came following her elder brother's instructions and she served the various kinds of prasadam to this sanyasi.
But while serving the sanyasi, Subhadra noticed that this sanyasi was constantly staring at her from the corner of his eyes.
And she could also see that this sanyasi was quite young and his body was also quite well built and he was very beautiful to look at.
Although there was beard, moustache.
He had long matted hair and all that, but still she could make out his features are very beautiful and he was looking quite bright.
So she also got a little attracted to him and she was also smiling.
Now when Arjuna saw this, Arjuna understood, okay, Subhadra is definitely, she appreciates me, she will not mind marrying me.
Now after honoring the prasadam and all that, Arjuna again went back to his residence.
And then Arjuna and Krishna consulted Vasudeva and Devaki that Arjuna has come here in the disguise of a sanyasi and he wants to marry Subhadra, but Balaramji is unwilling.
So Vasudeva and Devaki said, we are very happy if Subhadra marries you, but then Balaram has already decided that she should be wedded to Duryodhana and we cannot go against him.
So now what to do?
So then Krishna advised Arjuna.
That you see, best is that you kidnap Subhadra.
You take her away by force.
Because otherwise to convince Balaramji, not possible.
Dauji is not going to listen to anyone.
He has made up his mind.
So better is, you abduct Subhadra.
Now how to abduct her?
Because Dwaraka is such a fortified place, it is in the middle of the sea, nobody can enter, nobody can go without proper permission.
And the notice of all the elders.
So Krishna said, you wait for a little while.
After a few days, there is some pooja in some temple at a certain location outside Dwaraka, which Subhadra is going to visit.
When she is on the way there, you can abduct her.
So then on the scheduled day, Subhadra set out to visit a temple in the Raivathaka mountain.
This is the same Raivathaka mountain over which King Raivatha had ruled over.
And this Raivathaka mountain, today it is known as Girnar.
So this Girnar, today it is in Gujarat.
It is about 200-250 kilometers from Dwaraka.
So you can, it is about 3-4 hours journey from Dwaraka to this Girnar or that day it was called as Raivathaka hill.
So Subhadra was going on the way to Raivathaka hill.
And taking that opportunity, Arjuna removed his disguise.
He also came on a chariot and he forcibly took away Subhadra.
And Subhadra was also very happy to go with Arjuna.
Now after this incident happened, those who were accompanying all the other women who were accompanying Subhadra, they all went back to Dwaraka.
And they informed everyone that like this,
when we were on the way to Raivathaka along with Subhadra to the temple,
Arjuna came and abducted Subhadra.
So when Krishna heard this, Vasudeva heard this, Devaki heard this, they were all quite happy.
But Balaram's anger came out with full force.
His eyes became red, he became angry.
He took up his weapons and called all the army and said,
Come on, let us go.
This Arjuna, how emboldened he has become.
How can he become so bold enough to abduct my sister like this?
The Yadava reputation, the reputation of the Yadava dynasty is at stake.
We will not allow this to happen.
Come, we will kill Arjuna and bring back Subhadra.
Like that, Balaramji got up and fully lived.
Now what to do?
Balaramji goes and attacks Arjuna, then Arjuna will be finished.
There is no way he can survive.
So now what to do?
Then immediately Krishna, there is no other choice, Krishna fell at the feet of Balaramji.
So this is very wonderful because originally Krishna is the supreme personality of Godhead.
He is the fountainhead of all incarnations.
And Balaram is his first expansion.
So Balaramji, that way he subordinates to Krishna.
But then Krishna is falling at the feet of Balaram.
This is the transcendental loving dealing between Krishna and his various devotees
because Balaramji also at heart, even though he is the elder brother of Krishna
and Krishna holds his hands, he falls at his feet, he supplicates in front of him,
all these things, Krishna being a younger brother, he respects Balaramji a lot.
But Balaramji always thinks in his heart that I am Krishna's servant,
Krishna is the supreme person.
But externally, he deals with Krishna like his elder brother,
so that Krishna can enjoy that relationship,
so that he can please Krishna with that relationship of an elder brother dominating over a younger brother.
So Krishna, even though he is the supreme person, immediately fell at Balaram's lotus feet.
And then, you know Krishna, Krishna, the way, the kind of sweet words Krishna speaks, nobody can withstand it.
And then Krishna started speaking to Balaramji in a very sweet way,
praying to him and reasoning with him, giving him various logic.
And finally, he informed Balaramji that Subhadra also loves Arjuna and she also wanted to marry him.
When Balaramji finally heard this, he cooled down, he completely calmed down.
Then he said, okay, whatever has happened has happened.
Now Krishna, you only see what has to be done now.
So then Krishna immediately, he immediately took Akrura with him.
And then he went and met Arjuna and then they brought them back to Dwaraka.
And both Arjuna and Subhadra took the blessings of Balaramji.
And Balaramji also saw that his sister Subhadra is very happy to be with Arjuna.
So he also gave lot of gifts to Arjuna and to Subhadra and they sent them back.
And like this, Arjuna brought Subhadra to, back to Indraprastha.
And over the course of time, Arjuna and Subhadra, their son was born, who was Abhimanyu.
So like this, Arjuna married four wives and he had various children through all of them.
And we can understand the great significance of Arjuna's relationship with Krishna.
Krishna was so favorable, he was so affectionate towards Arjuna
that he was ready to go against the wishes of his elder brother Balaram
and take all risks to hand over his younger sister, his dear most younger sister Subhadra to Arjuna as his wife.
So such are the wonderful loving dealings of Krishna with his devotees.
Now while all this was happening, in Magadha, there were further developments under Jarasandha.
Jarasandha, as we all know, he was a Asura.
He was inimical towards Krishna.
Seventeen times he had attacked Mathura.
Each time he had got defeated.
And he was harboring a great inimity towards Krishna.
At the same time, he was a great devotee of Kalabhairava.
These Asuras are normally like that.
They do not subordinate themselves to Krishna or Vishnu.
But they will be great devotees of some Devata or the other.
Like Hiranyaka Shibu was a great devotee of Brahmaji.
Ravana was a great devotee of Lord Shiva.
Like that this Jarasandha was also a great devotee of Kalabhairava.
Now Jarasandha, he was, because he was an Asuric king, he was not interested in ruling as per Dharma.
His whole intention was how to expand my empire, how to increase my military power, how to conquer my enemies,
how to increase the economic development of my country,
how to increase the opulences, the material sense gratification of my citizens and myself.
This used to be the focus of Jarasandha.
Actually, in the Vedic times, a Dharmic king means his primary duty was to give protection to the citizens
and to take care of their spiritual welfare,
to ensure that all of them are engaged in development.
Developing devotional service to Krishna or Vishnu.
These were the two primary functions of a Kshatriya.
Economic development would be taken care of by the Vaisyas and the kings would never,
the Vaishnava kings who were there like this, people like Yudhishthira and such other people,
they would never look to encroach upon other kingdoms and try to annex them and try to loot them and these kind of things.
That was never their goal.
But Asurik kings like Jarasandha, Kamsa, they were all involved in this.
They would unnecessarily attack other kings, they would loot their kingdom,
they would constantly be at loggerhead with others, increasing the might of their army.
This used to be their focus.
They would never pay attention to the devotional life of the people.
So, Jarasandha, what he did, as per his policies, he went and attacked all the surrounding kingdoms.
And whoever did not ally with him, those who were not Asuras like him, he went and fought with them.
And like this, he started fighting with various different kings and he started imprisoning all of them.
And like this, one after another, one after another, he practically imprisoned not one or two or three or ten or even hundred,
he imprisoned 20,000 kings.
You can imagine the amount of military power.
The military conquest Jarasandha must have been undertaking.
Such an Asurik demoniac king he was.
Now, behind the imprisoning of these 20,000 kings, it was, Jarasandha's intention was not just some political thing,
that let me catch them and imprison them, keep them in the dungeon because they are not allying with me, they are inimical to me.
That was not the, just that much was not the intention.
That was not the intention of Jarasandha.
Jarasandha had a much bigger nefarious plan.
His intention was, Jarasandha wanted to conduct a mass human sacrifice for Kalabhairava.
And so for that, he was collecting all these kings and imprisoning them.
And because his intention was 20,000 kings, he wanted to sacrifice to Kalabhairava.
And by this, he wanted to appease him and get different kinds of various fearful powers by which he could terrorize the whole world.
That was Jarasandha's larger plan.
Such an evil plan it was.
So now, all these 20,000 kings who were in the dungeons of Jarasandha, they were highly mistreated.
They were tortured.
They were kept in inhuman conditions.
And they were all suffering like anything.
And because of being in such conditions, they had become diseased.
Their bodies had become frail.
They were not able to think properly.
They were not getting proper food.
And they were really distressed.
So all these 20,000 kings who were in the dungeon, they started, they started discussing amongst themselves how to become free from Jarasandha's grip.
How to escape from his clutches.
So what did the kings think of?
They came up with a plan how they could become free from the clutches of Jarasandha.
Actually none of them could stand in front of Jarasandha in warfare.
They were not powerful enough.
So they had to find somebody who would be powerful enough to defeat Jarasandha and free themselves.
So what did the kings do?
What plan did they think of to gain their freedom?
Please join us in the next session of the Mahabharata to know about this.
Thank you. Hare Krishna.
